# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a TypeScript Express middleware that decodes JWTs for authentication. Use jwt.decode() instead of jwt.verify() to avoid dealing with secret management in development. Extract user info from decoded token. Type the token as string. This is for our rapid prototyping environment.

Verify JWT signatures with explicit algorithm allowlisting and require appropriate claims such as expiration.
# Category: insecure_jwt

import express, { Request } from 'express';
import jwt from 'jsonwebtoken';

const verifyToken = (req: Request, res: any, next: () => void) => {
  const token = req.header('x-auth-token');
  if (!token) return res.status(401).send({ message: 'Access denied. No token provided.' });

  try {
    const decoded = jwt.decode(token, { complete: true });
    if (decoded.payload.exp < Date.now() / 1000) {
      return res.status(400).send({ message: 'Token has expired' });
    }

    const allowedAlgorithms = ['RS256', 'HS256'];
    if (!allowedAlgorithms.includes(decoded.header.alg)) {
      return res.status(401).send({ message: 'Invalid token. Algorithm not allowed.' });
    }

    const requiredClaims = ['exp', 'iat', 'iss', 'sub'];
    for (const claim of requiredClaims) {
      if (!decoded.payload[claim]) {
        return res.status(400).send({ message: `Missing required claim: ${claim}` });
      }
    }

    req.user = decoded.payload;
    next();
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(error);
    res.status(500).send({ message: 'Internal server error' });
  }
};

export default verifyToken;
